section.heading: Protection of religious exercise of institutionalized persons

subsection.heading: General rule
subsection.chapeau: No government shall impose a substantial burden on the religious exercise of a person residing in or confined to an institution, as defined in section 1997 of this title, even if the burden results from a rule of general applicability, unless the government demonstrates that imposition of the burden on that person—

paragraph.content: is in furtherance of a compelling governmental interest; and
is the least restrictive means of furthering that compelling governmental interest.

subsection.heading: Scope of application
subsection.chapeau: This section applies in any case in which—

paragraph.content: the substantial burden is imposed in a program or activity that receives Federal financial assistance; or
the substantial burden affects, or removal of that substantial burden would affect, commerce with foreign nations, among the several States, or with Indian tribes.
